---
title: "NBA Total Points Prediction: Predictive Modeling"
author: "Maya Lennon"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
```

## Library Loading

```{r libraries}
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(caret)
library(glmnet)
library(rpart)
library(rpart.plot)
library(randomForest)
library(gbm)
library(nnet)
library(Metrics)
library(tibble)
library(lubridate)
library(purrr)
```

```{r}
model_ready <- read_csv("TOTAL_MODEL_READY.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  filter(game_date >= as.Date("2013-10-01"))

cat("Total rows:", nrow(model_ready), "\n")
cat("Date range:", as.character(min(model_ready$game_date)),
    "to", as.character(max(model_ready$game_date)), "\n")
```

## TP Dist.

```{r}
summary(model_ready$total)
cat("Skewness:", round(e1071::skewness(model_ready$total), 3), "\n")
cat("Kurtosis:", round(e1071::kurtosis(model_ready$total), 3), "\n")


par(mfrow = c(1, 3))

hist(model_ready$total,
     breaks = 50,
     main   = "Distribution of Total Points",
     xlab   = "Total Points",
     col    = "#2E75B6", border = "white")

hist(log(model_ready$total),
     breaks = 50,
     main   = "Distribution of log(Total Points)",
     xlab   = "log(Total Points)",
     col    = "#2E75B6", border = "white")

qqnorm(model_ready$total, main = "QQ Plot: Total Points")
qqline(model_ready$total, col = "red")

par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
```


## Train and testing data

```{r}
cutoff_date <- as.Date("2025-03-13")

train <- model_ready |> filter(game_date <  cutoff_date)
test  <- model_ready|> filter(game_date >= cutoff_date)

cat("Training set:", nrow(train), "games (",
    as.character(min(train$game_date)), "to",
    as.character(max(train$game_date)), ")\n")
cat("Test set:    ", nrow(test),  "games (",
    as.character(min(test$game_date)),  "to",
    as.character(max(test$game_date)),  ")\n")
```
```{r}
library(corrplot)
```

## Feature matrix

```{r}

id_cols  <- c("gameId", "game_date", "home_team", "away_team", "gameType", "total")
feat_cols <- setdiff(names(model_ready), id_cols)

cat("Number of predictors:", length(feat_cols), "\n")
cat("Predictors:\n")
print(feat_cols)

X_train <- train[, feat_cols]
y_train <- train$total

X_test  <- test[, feat_cols]
y_test  <- test$total

y_train_log <- log(y_train)
y_test_log  <- log(y_test)

na_counts <- colSums(is.na(X_train))
if (any(na_counts > 0)) {
  cat("WARNING — NAs in training features:\n")
  print(na_counts[na_counts > 0])
} else {
  cat("No NAs in training feature matrix.\n")
}

numeric_data <- model_ready |>
  select(where(is.numeric)) |>
  select(-total)
cor_matrix <- cor(numeric_data, use = "complete.obs")
round(cor_matrix, 2)
corrplot(cor_matrix, method = "color", type = "upper")
cor_with_total <- sapply(numeric_data, function(x)
  cor(model_ready$total, x, use = "complete.obs"))

sort(abs(cor_with_total), decreasing = TRUE)

lm_test <- lm(
  total ~ .,
  data = model_ready |>
    select(total, all_of(names(numeric_data)))
)

vif(lm_test)

```
## Cross-validation

```{r}
# Created a time-series cross-validation using caret's timeslice method. First 60% of training data as initial training, each validation window at 8% of training data. Then did 5-fold CV, compute MAE functions fo rlater, create correlation matrix
ts_ctrl <- trainControl(
  method          = "timeslice",
  initialWindow  = floor(nrow(train) * 0.6),   
  horizon        = floor(nrow(train) * 0.08),  
  fixedWindow    = FALSE,                       
  summaryFunction = defaultSummary,
  allowParallel  = TRUE
)

cv5_ctrl <- trainControl(
  method   = "cv",
  number   = 5,
  summaryFunction = defaultSummary
)

mae_raw <- function(actual, predicted) {
  mean(abs(actual - predicted))
}

mae_log_backtransform <- function(actual, pred_log) {
  mean(abs(actual - exp(pred_log)))
}


```


## Baseline Model

```{r}
baseline_pred <- rep(mean(y_train), length(y_test))
baseline_mae  <- mae_raw(y_test, baseline_pred)

cat("Baseline (training mean prediction):", round(mean(y_train), 2), "points\n")
cat("Baseline MAE on test set:", round(baseline_mae, 2), "\n")
```

## Model 1: Ordinary Least Squares Linear Regression
o l s l o l

### Full Model 1

```{r}
lm_full <- lm(total ~ ., data = cbind(total = y_train, X_train))
lm_full_pred <- predict(lm_full, newdata = X_test)
lm_full_mae  <- mae_raw(y_test, lm_full_pred)

cat("OLS Full Model — Test MAE:", round(lm_full_mae, 2), "\n")
cat("R-squared (training):", round(summary(lm_full)$r.squared, 4), "\n")

# Check for high VIF (multicollinearity)
if (requireNamespace("car", quietly = TRUE)) {
  vif_vals <- car::vif(lm_full)
  cat("\nPredictors with VIF > 10 (multicollinearity concern):\n")
  print(vif_vals[vif_vals > 10])
}
```

### Pruned OLS Model 1
```{r}
pruned_vars <- c(
  "blend_total_proj5",
  "pace_pressure5",
  "three_point_env5",
  "free_throw_env5",
  "tov_env5",
  "shooting_env5",
  "over230_env10",
  "rest_diff_capped",
  "any_back_to_back",
  "is_playoff",
  "recent_total_volatility"
)

lm_pruned <- lm(total ~ .,
                data = cbind(total = y_train,
                             X_train[, pruned_vars]))
lm_pruned_pred <- predict(lm_pruned, newdata = X_test[, pruned_vars])
lm_pruned_mae  <- mae_raw(y_test, lm_pruned_pred)

cat("OLS Pruned Model — Test MAE:", round(lm_pruned_mae, 2), "\n")
cat("Pruned R-squared (training):", round(summary(lm_pruned)$r.squared, 4), "\n")
print(summary(lm_pruned))
```

### OLS on Log-Transformed Total Model 1
```{r}
lm_log <- lm(log(total) ~ .,
             data = cbind(total = y_train_log,
                          X_train[, pruned_vars]))
lm_log_pred_log <- predict(lm_log, newdata = X_test[, pruned_vars])
lm_log_pred_raw <- exp(lm_log_pred_log)
lm_log_mae      <- mae_raw(y_test, lm_log_pred_raw)

cat("OLS Log-Transformed — Test MAE (back-transformed):", round(lm_log_mae, 2), "\n")
```



## Model 2: Polynomial Regression
```{r}
poly_vars_linear <- setdiff(pruned_vars, c("blend_total_proj5", "pace_pressure5",
                                            "three_point_env5", "tov_env5",
                                            "shooting_env5"))

train_poly <- cbind(
  total               = y_train,
  X_train[, poly_vars_linear],
  poly(X_train$blend_total_proj5, 2, raw = TRUE) |>
    as.data.frame() |> setNames(c("blend5_d1", "blend5_d2")),
  poly(X_train$pace_pressure5,    2, raw = TRUE) |>
    as.data.frame() |> setNames(c("pace5_d1",  "pace5_d2")),
  poly(X_train$three_point_env5,  2, raw = TRUE) |>
    as.data.frame() |> setNames(c("tp5_d1",    "tp5_d2")),
  poly(X_train$tov_env5,          2, raw = TRUE) |>
    as.data.frame() |> setNames(c("tov5_d1",   "tov5_d2")),
  poly(X_train$shooting_env5,     2, raw = TRUE) |>
    as.data.frame() |> setNames(c("shoot5_d1", "shoot5_d2"))
)

test_poly <- cbind(
  X_test[, poly_vars_linear],
  poly(X_test$blend_total_proj5, 2, raw = TRUE) |>
    as.data.frame() |> setNames(c("blend5_d1", "blend5_d2")),
  poly(X_test$pace_pressure5,    2, raw = TRUE) |>
    as.data.frame() |> setNames(c("pace5_d1",  "pace5_d2")),
  poly(X_test$three_point_env5,  2, raw = TRUE) |>
    as.data.frame() |> setNames(c("tp5_d1",    "tp5_d2")),
  poly(X_test$tov_env5,          2, raw = TRUE) |>
    as.data.frame() |> setNames(c("tov5_d1",   "tov5_d2")),
  poly(X_test$shooting_env5,     2, raw = TRUE) |>
    as.data.frame() |> setNames(c("shoot5_d1", "shoot5_d2"))
)

lm_poly <- lm(total ~ ., data = train_poly)
lm_poly_pred <- predict(lm_poly, newdata = test_poly)
lm_poly_mae  <- mae_raw(y_test, lm_poly_pred)

cat("Polynomial Regression (degree 2) — Test MAE:", round(lm_poly_mae, 2), "\n")
cat("Polynomial R-squared (training):", round(summary(lm_poly)$r.squared, 4), "\n")

# Check which squared terms are significant
poly_coefs <- summary(lm_poly)$coefficients
cat("\nSquared-term significance (p-values):\n")
print(round(poly_coefs[grep("_d2", rownames(poly_coefs)), 4], 4))
```


## Model 3: Gradient Boosted Trees (GBM)
hardest model - takes long

```{r}
gbm_grid <- expand.grid(
  n.trees           = c(500, 1000, 1500),
  interaction.depth = c(2, 4, 6),
  shrinkage         = c(0.05, 0.1),
  n.minobsinnode    = 20
)

set.seed(42)
gbm_tune <- train(
  x         = X_train,
  y         = y_train,
  method    = "gbm",
  metric    = "MAE",
  trControl = trainControl(method = "cv", number = 5, verboseIter = FALSE),
  tuneGrid  = gbm_grid,
  verbose   = FALSE
)

best_gbm_params <- gbm_tune$bestTune
cat("=== Best GBM Hyperparameters (5-fold CV) ===\n")
print(best_gbm_params)

# Refit on full training set 
set.seed(42)
gbm_final <- gbm(
  formula           = total ~ .,
  data              = cbind(total = y_train, X_train),
  distribution      = "gaussian",
  n.trees           = best_gbm_params$n.trees,
  interaction.depth = best_gbm_params$interaction.depth,
  shrinkage         = best_gbm_params$shrinkage,
  n.minobsinnode    = best_gbm_params$n.minobsinnode,
  bag.fraction      = 0.75,
  train.fraction    = 1.0,
  verbose           = FALSE
)

gbm_pred <- predict(gbm_final,
                    newdata  = X_test,
                    n.trees  = best_gbm_params$n.trees)
gbm_mae  <- mae_raw(y_test, gbm_pred)

cat("GBM Final Model — Test MAE:", round(gbm_mae, 2), "\n")

# who is importance let's see, then visualize
cat("\nGBM — Variable Importance (top 15):\n")
gbm_summary <- summary(gbm_final, n.trees = best_gbm_params$n.trees,
                       plotit = FALSE)
print(head(gbm_summary, 15))

barplot(head(gbm_summary$rel.inf, 15),
        names.arg = head(gbm_summary$var, 15),
        horiz     = TRUE,
        las       = 2,
        main      = "GBM Relative Influence (Top 15)",
        xlab      = "Relative Influence (%)",
        col       = "#2E75B6",
        cex.names = 0.7)
#same seed for predictability
set.seed(42)
gbm_log_final <- gbm(
  formula           = total_log ~ .,
  data              = cbind(total_log = y_train_log, X_train),
  distribution      = "gaussian",
  n.trees           = best_gbm_params$n.trees,
  interaction.depth = best_gbm_params$interaction.depth,
  shrinkage         = best_gbm_params$shrinkage,
  n.minobsinnode    = best_gbm_params$n.minobsinnode,
  bag.fraction      = 0.75,
  train.fraction    = 1.0,
  verbose           = FALSE
)

gbm_log_pred <- exp(predict(gbm_log_final,
                             newdata = X_test,
                             n.trees = best_gbm_params$n.trees))
gbm_log_mae  <- mae_raw(y_test, gbm_log_pred)
cat("GBM (log scale, back-transformed) — Test MAE:", round(gbm_log_mae, 2), "\n")
```

## Model selection

```{r}
results_table <- tibble(
  Model = c(
    "Baseline (training mean)",
    "OLS — Full",
    "OLS — Pruned",
    "OLS — Pruned (log scale)",
    "Polynomial Regression (degree 2)",
    "GBM (raw)",
    "GBM (log scale)"
  ),
  Test_MAE = c(
    baseline_mae,
    lm_full_mae,
    lm_pruned_mae,
    lm_log_mae,
    lm_poly_mae,
    best_enet$test_mae,
    enet_log_mae,
    tree_mae,
    rf_mae,
    gbm_mae,
    gbm_log_mae,
    nnet_mae
  )
) |>
  arrange(Test_MAE) |>
  mutate(
    Test_MAE      = round(Test_MAE, 3),
    Improvement_vs_Baseline = round(baseline_mae - Test_MAE, 3)
  )

cat("model comps, sorted by\n")
print(results_table, n = Inf)

best_model_name <- results_table$Model[1]
best_model_mae  <- results_table$Test_MAE[1]
cat("\nBest model:", best_model_name, "| Test MAE:", best_model_mae, "\n")
```

```{r}
#make it cute
ggplot(results_table,
       aes(x = reorder(Model, -Test_MAE), y = Test_MAE, fill = Test_MAE)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = baseline_mae, linetype = "dashed",
             color = "red", size = 0.8) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#1F6B3A", high = "#D94F3D") +
  labs(
    title    = "Model comp: test set MAE",
    subtitle = "Red dashed line = baseline (training mean). Lower is better.",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Mean Absolute Error (points)",
    fill     = "MAE"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```


## best model

```{r}
# Determine which GBM to use
best_gbm_mae  <- min(gbm_mae, gbm_log_mae)
use_log_scale <- gbm_log_mae < gbm_mae
cat("Best GBM version:", if (use_log_scale) "Log scale" else "Raw scale", "\n")
cat("Best GBM Test MAE:", round(best_gbm_mae, 2), "\n")

print("GBM\n")
cat("n.trees:           ", best_gbm_params$n.trees, "\n")
cat("interaction.depth: ", best_gbm_params$interaction.depth, "\n")
cat("shrinkage:         ", best_gbm_params$shrinkage, "\n")
cat("n.minobsinnode:    ", best_gbm_params$n.minobsinnode, "\n")
cat("bag.fraction:      ", 0.75, "\n")
cat("distribution:       gaussian\n")
cat("Response scale:    ", if (use_log_scale) "log(total)" else "total", "\n")


final_best_model <- if (use_log_scale) gbm_log_final else gbm_final
best_pred_test <- if (use_log_scale) gbm_log_pred else gbm_pred
residuals_test <- y_test - best_pred_test

par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
plot(best_pred_test, residuals_test,
     xlab = "Predicted Total",
     ylab = "Residual (Actual - Predicted)",
     main = "Residuals vs. Fitted (Test Set)",
     pch  = 16, col = rgb(0.18, 0.46, 0.71, 0.5), cex = 0.6)
abline(h = 0, col = "red", lty = 2)

hist(residuals_test, breaks = 40,
     main = "Residual Distribution (Test Set)",
     xlab = "Residual", col = "#2E75B6", border = "white")
abline(v = 0, col = "red", lty = 2)
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

cat("\nResidual Summary (Test Set):\n")
cat("Mean residual:", round(mean(residuals_test), 3), "(bias)\n")
cat("SD of residuals:", round(sd(residuals_test), 3), "\n")

```
